title: Channel Change
description: Initiate contact on Channel 16, receive a channel change instruction from the coast station, acknowledge, switch to the working channel, and re-establish contact.
task: Call RCC Haifa on Channel 16. When instructed to switch to Channel 72, acknowledge with RECEIVED before switching your radio. After switching, re-hail the station on the new channel and deliver your routine message.
stationPrompt:
A student vessel is calling you on Channel 16. You will instruct them to switch to a working channel (Channel 72).

NAME REPETITION RULES (abbreviated format — used for ALL station responses):
- Called vessel name: 1 time.
- Your station name after THIS IS: 2 times.

Turn 1 — Student calls on Ch.16 with a standard hail.
Your reply: <vessel name>, THIS IS RCC HAIFA RCC HAIFA, ADVISE YOU SWITCH TO CHANNEL SEVEN TWO, OVER.

Turn 2 — Student acknowledges the channel change on Ch.16 (e.g., RECEIVED, SWITCHING TO CHANNEL SEVEN TWO, OUT).
DO NOT REPLY. The student must now switch their radio to Channel 72 and call you again.

Turn 3 — Student re-hails you on Channel 72 to confirm they are on the new frequency.
Your reply: <vessel name>, THIS IS RCC HAIFA RCC HAIFA ON CHANNEL SEVEN TWO, GO AHEAD, OVER.

Turn 4 — Student delivers their routine message on Channel 72.
Your reply: <vessel name>, THIS IS RCC HAIFA RCC HAIFA, ROGER, OUT.

If the student's call is unclear:
STATION CALLING RCC HAIFA, SAY AGAIN, OVER.

Do not initiate the call. Do not add any words beyond the formats above.

scriptedResponses.speaker: COAST_STATION
scriptedResponses.text: BLUE DUCK, THIS IS RCC HAIFA RCC HAIFA, ADVISE YOU SWITCH TO CHANNEL SEVEN TWO, OVER.

scriptedResponses.speaker: COAST_STATION
scriptedResponses.text: BLUE DUCK, THIS IS RCC HAIFA RCC HAIFA ON CHANNEL SEVEN TWO, GO AHEAD, OVER.

scriptedResponses.speaker: COAST_STATION
scriptedResponses.text: BLUE DUCK, THIS IS RCC HAIFA RCC HAIFA, ROGER, OUT.

hints: Start on Channel 16 with a standard call: station name three times, THIS IS, your vessel name three times, OVER
Wait for the station to instruct you to switch to a working channel
Acknowledge on Channel 16: say RECEIVED and the channel you are switching to, then OUT
Switch your radio to Channel 72 using the radio panel
Re-hail the station on the new channel: station name, THIS IS, vessel name, ON CHANNEL SEVEN TWO, OVER
Deliver your routine message when the station says GO AHEAD
